title: hibbertia squarrosa
intro: Hibbertia squarrosa, also known as Guinea flower, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may be beneficial for wound healing and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, Hibbertia squarrosa is often used in topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as in teas and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
